ASI01.name: Prompt Injection
ASI01.description: Adversarial inputs injected via tool descriptions, user data, or external content manipulate agent reasoning and override intended behaviour.

ASI02.name: Sensitive Data Exposure
ASI02.description: Agents inadvertently expose confidential data — credentials, PII, proprietary content — through outputs, logs, or tool responses.

ASI03.name: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
ASI03.description: Malicious or compromised plugins, tools, models, or dependencies introduce backdoors or exfiltration paths into the agent stack.

ASI04.name: Insecure Direct Tool Invocation
ASI04.description: Agents invoke tools without adequate authorisation checks, allowing privilege escalation or unintended system access.

ASI05.name: Improper Output Handling / SSRF
ASI05.description: Agent outputs are passed to downstream systems without sanitisation, enabling SSRF, injection, or unauthorised network access.

ASI06.name: Excessive Agency
ASI06.description: Agents are granted more permissions, network access, or action scope than needed, amplifying the blast radius of compromise.

ASI07.name: Insecure Cryptography
ASI07.description: Use of weak, deprecated, or misapplied cryptographic algorithms undermines confidentiality and integrity guarantees.
ASI07.note: No findings in this scan — detectors active

ASI08.name: Insecure Deserialization
ASI08.description: Deserializing untrusted data with unsafe libraries (pickle, yaml.load, ObjectInputStream) enables remote code execution.

ASI09.name: Improper Error Handling / Path Traversal
ASI09.description: Insufficient input validation allows path traversal attacks giving agents access to files outside the intended scope.

ASI10.name: Inadequate Audit and Monitoring
ASI10.description: Absence of comprehensive logging for agent actions, tool calls, and sampling events prevents detection and forensic analysis.
ASI10.note: No findings in this scan — detectors active
